key_insights.insight: Responding positively to bids for connection is crucial for a successful relationship, with successful couples turning towards each other 85% of the time.
key_insights.quote: The couples who were successful in the long haulturned towards each other's little bits for connection 85% of the time the couples who ended up splitting up unhappy divorced 33 percent of thetime so listen to that difference just between saying uh-huh and saying nothing.

key_insights.insight: Empathy is a powerful tool for creating connection; attuning to a partner's feelings and needs fosters deeper understanding and compassion.
key_insights.quote: Empathy is super, super important.And I honestly believe that empathy is probably the most powerful tool we have to really create connection with one another.

key_insights.insight: Expressing needs positively, focusing on what you want your partner to do rather than what they're doing wrong, is more effective in achieving desired outcomes.
key_insights.quote: Flip that on its head and say, I would love it if you would help me tonight with the dishes.

key_insights.insight: The key to managing conflict effectively lies in understanding your partner's perspective before attempting to solve the problem.
key_insights.quote: If we apply the dictum of really understanding our partner and their point of view before we work on trying to resolve the problem, we're going to do much better.

key_insights.insight: During conflict, maintaining a ratio of five positive interactions to one negative interaction is crucial for a healthy relationship.
key_insights.quote: That ratio of positivity to negativity was five to one or higher amongrelationships that work well.

key_insights.insight: Contempt is the most corrosive of the 'Four Horsemen' in relationships, predicting breakup more accurately than other negative behaviors.
key_insights.quote: The third one was contempt.And that was the worst.That was the best predictor of relationship breakup of all.

key_insights.insight: During arguments, describe your own feelings and needs, rather than describing your partner and what's wrong with them, as criticism sabotages effective communication.
key_insights.quote: Trytelling your partner what you feel and what you need instead of describing your partner and what's wrong with him.

key_insights.insight: Men who kiss their wives goodbye in the morning live significantly longer than those who don't, indicating the power of small affectionate gestures.
key_insights.quote: Men who kiss their wives goodbye when they leave forwork live four years longer than men who don't

key_insights.insight: The way an argument starts is the way it will go 96% of the time, meaning that first impressions, even in conflict, really matter.
key_insights.quote: The way an argument startsis the way it'll go 96% of the time.

key_insights.insight: The only way to be truly powerful in a relationship is by being influenceable and accepting influence from your partner.
key_insights.quote: The only way to be powerful in a relationshipis to accept influence and it's so counterintuitive,but that turns out to be really powerful.

actionable_advice.advice: Create rituals of connection to stay attuned and avoid assumptions.
actionable_advice.how_to: Establish daily check-ins, asking about each other's day and feelings, and create weekly rituals like asking 'What can I do this week to make you feel loved?'

actionable_advice.advice: Take a time out during arguments if you feel flooded.
actionable_advice.how_to: Communicate the need for a break, specify a return time, engage in self-soothing activities (reading, working out), and avoid dwelling on the argument.

actionable_advice.advice: Use the 'Bagel Method' to compromise on perpetual problems.
actionable_advice.how_to: Identify what you cannot compromise on (inner circle) and what you are flexible about (outer circle). Share these with your partner and find compromises within the flexible areas.

actionable_advice.advice: Utilize structured conversations to improve sexual intimacy
actionable_advice.how_to: Use 'Got Sex' or a similar kit that prompts conversations about preferences, initiation, refusal and completion of sex. Ask the 'Love Map' 100 question sets to better know each other's erotic world.

actionable_advice.advice: If your partner says 'I need to take a break'.
actionable_advice.how_to: Accept it, give them space and ensure that you come back after 30 minutes, an hour or 24 hours to revisit the topic.

memorable_stories: Julie described John's upbringing in a small apartment and his ability to fully concentrate which at first felt rejecting but later was understood. Shows the need for empathy.
Julie described her method of asking John to clean up and how she escalates the ask to the point where she ends up wrestling him (jokingly) to get him to comply. Illustrates perpetual problems.

Julie and John recounted a story about seeing a therapist that was training other therapists and allowing them to have Sheila summarize and empathize with Harry, after Harry insulted and put her down. Demonstrates bad examples in therapy.

John described the experience with his parents as a child and how he was so loved and his sister was very unloved and the way they treated her, musically, was something he didn't agree with. Shares light on his origin story.
They described a story of a lawyer that asked what makes his wife the most angry and the wife responded that she felt he sounded too authoritarian and he said that makes sense and was receptive.
contrarian_claims: 69% of relationship problems are perpetual and unsolvable, so the key is management, not resolution.
Opposites attract, meaning that successful couples don't need to have the same dreams, passions, or interests.
Familiarity, not novelty, is the basis for eroticism in long-term relationships.
Couples do not need to align on dreams and it can still work.
Going to sleep angry is okay if it is late at night.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Gottman Doctors: "Women Tend to Be More Unhappily Married", 96% Of Non-Cuddlers Have An Awful Sex Life & Why One Night Stands Are Dangerous!
